desktop.availableDesc: Download it when you're ready. OpenSquilla will install it only after you relaunch.
desktop.manualAvailableDesc: Download the versioned Windows installer. Windows will ask you to complete the installation.
desktop.manualDownloadedDesc: OpenSquilla {version} was verified against the canonical GitHub checksum and shown in its folder. Run the installer when ready.
desktop.downloadedDesc: Relaunch OpenSquilla to finish updating to {version}.
desktop.downloadingDesc: Keep OpenSquilla open while the update downloads.
desktop.applyingDesc: OpenSquilla is preparing to relaunch and install the update.
desktop.errorSourceUnreachable: The update sources are temporarily unreachable. Try again later.
desktop.errorManifestInvalid: The release-channel data is invalid, so OpenSquilla cannot safely confirm an update.
desktop.errorChecksumUnavailable: The installer cannot be verified because the canonical GitHub checksum is unavailable. No installer was opened.
desktop.errorIntegrityFailed: The downloaded installer failed integrity verification and was deleted.
desktop.errorDownloadFailed: OpenSquilla could not download the update. Check your connection and try again.
desktop.errorInstallFailed: OpenSquilla could not prepare or open the installer. Try again or use the release page.
desktop.idleDesc: Check for a new desktop release.
desktop.manualIdleDesc: Check for a new desktop release. Windows updates open a versioned installer for manual installation.
desktop.unsupported: Native desktop updates are unavailable on this platform. Use the release link when a new version is announced.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: This address doesn't match an OpenSquilla page.

chrome.sidebarWidthLimitedValue: Sidebar width: {width}px, {preset}; limited by this window (preferred {preference}px)

bgm.label: Background music
bgm.noTracks: No tracks bundled — add files to music/ or pick a local file

chrome.moreThemesHint: Additional themes are available in Appearance settings

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.desc: Theme, language, and browser-local interface preferences. Presets apply instantly; custom widths use Apply.
appearance.themeDesc: Follow your system, or force light or dark. The sidebar toggle is a shortcut to this same setting.
appearance.languageDesc: Display language for the interface. Applies instantly to this browser.
appearance.toolDetailsDesc: Auto follows the tool type; Compact collapses ordinary tools; Expanded opens them. Errors open automatically by default and can still be collapsed.
appearance.bgmDesc: Show the background-music control in the top bar. Off by default; also toggleable from the command palette.
appearance.sidebarWidthDesc: Choose the desktop sidebar width. Compact and drawer layouts use a fixed width.
appearance.sidebarWidthCustomHint: Use the minus and plus buttons or enter a value, then apply it.
appearance.sidebarWidthRange: Enter a whole number from {min} to {max}.
appearance.sidebarWidthCompactStatus: Compact layout uses {current}px. Your desktop preference of {saved}px is preserved.
appearance.sidebarWidthDrawerStatus: Drawer layout uses {current}px. Your desktop preference of {saved}px is preserved.

dataMigration.title: Data & backups
dataMigration.desc: Find and review data from another OpenSquilla installation. Nothing is scanned until you open this page, and nothing is changed without your confirmation.
dataMigration.unsupportedDesc: This gateway or Desktop shell does not support the private preview API yet. Update the host, then return to this page.
dataMigration.invalidResponse: The gateway returned an unsupported migration preview.
dataMigration.compatibilityDesc: OpenSquilla is using a compatible legacy Agent data location. Changing it switches the identity and memory source; it does not move, merge, or delete data.
dataMigration.recoveryTitle: Import is unavailable in a recovery profile
dataMigration.loadFailed: OpenSquilla could not inspect import sources.
dataMigration.emptyDesc: You can scan again later. Desktop can also choose a data folder manually.
dataMigration.chooseSourceDesc: Review a source before importing. Sources remain unchanged and are never merged with current data.
dataMigration.webReadOnly: This web connection can preview the import only. Complete it in Desktop or on the gateway host.
dataMigration.hostCliHint: To complete the import from a terminal, run the migration tool on the gateway host. No server path is exposed in this browser.

blockers.source_in_use: The source is currently in use.
blockers.source_schema_newer: The source was created by a newer OpenSquilla version.
blockers.source_database_invalid: The source database could not be validated.
blockers.source_config_invalid: The source configuration could not be validated.
blockers.source_unreadable: The source could not be read safely.
blockers.insufficient_disk: There is not enough free disk space.
blockers.target_recovery_required: The current data must be recovered before importing.
blockers.target_not_replaceable: The current data cannot be safely replaced.
blockers.preview_unavailable: A safe preview is not available for this source.

notices.whole_profile_replacement: The current profile will be backed up and replaced as a whole; data is not merged.
notices.previously_imported: This source was imported previously and is still available for review.
notices.scheduled_jobs_will_be_paused: Imported scheduled jobs remain paused until you review them.

dialog.title: Settings
dialog.applyLiveNote: Most changes apply live; some need a gateway restart
dialog.discardBody: You have unsaved edits. Closing now will lose them.

onboarding.provider: Couldn't save the provider — check the model and credentials
onboarding.router: Couldn't save the router — check the mode and tiers
onboarding.search: Couldn't save the search provider — check the settings
onboarding.channel: Couldn't save the channel — check the fields
onboarding.image: Couldn't save image generation — check the model, size, and format

cli.hintBundled: These commands use the CLI bundled with the app; paste into a terminal to run.
cli.hintDev: Dev mode: commands run from the source checkout via uv.
cli.restartHint: Use the app's Settings → Runtime → Restart to apply.

status.envNotVisible: {capability} is selected, but ${envKey} is not visible to the gateway.

action.reviewPending: Review setup sections for pending actions

provider.title: Provider
provider.pageDesc: Connect model providers and manage credentials, endpoints, and the direct/fallback model. Choose how models are used in Model Routing.
provider.configuredSummary: {ready} of {count} credentials ready. Verification sends one small model request and may incur provider charges.
provider.configuredDesc: Saved providers remain available for routing until you remove them. Configuration verification sends a small model request using the saved settings; provider charges may apply.
provider.configuredEmpty: Connect a provider to verify credentials and choose available models.
provider.emptyRoutingHint: Once connected, you can set up intelligent routing; one provider can still route between multiple models.
provider.catalogDesc: Choose a provider. Its configuration will open directly below.
provider.routerStateCustom: Custom · preserved when the active provider changes
provider.routerStateLegacy: Existing configuration · preserved when the active provider changes
provider.routingStatusDesc: {count} providers saved · {ready} ready. Adding providers does not enable Routing or Ensemble.
provider.routingStatusDescOne: 1 provider saved · {ready} ready. Adding a provider does not enable Routing or Ensemble.
provider.routingOnDesc: Model Routing can use your configured providers.
provider.routingOffDesc: Providers stay saved while routing is off. Enable it when you want to route across models.
provider.modelUsageRouterDesc: Choose one suitable configured model for each request.
provider.modelUsageEnsembleDesc: Generate with multiple models, then combine their results.
provider.modelUsageFixedDesc: Send every request to the current direct model.
provider.addKeyToTestHint: Add an API key before verifying this provider. You can verify an unsaved pasted key.
provider.addModelToTestHint: Choose a model before verifying this provider.
provider.statusUnavailableTestHint: Provider status is unavailable, so the saved configuration cannot be verified yet.
provider.activationModelRequired: Choose and save a model below before making this provider active.
provider.activationModelRequiredHint: Choose a direct/fallback model in this provider's editor and save it first.
provider.activationPoolUnsupported: This provider uses a key pool and cannot be made active. It remains available to Routing and Ensemble.
provider.activationStatusUnavailable: Provider activation status is unavailable. Update and restart the Gateway.
provider.activationUnavailable: This provider cannot be made active with its saved configuration.
provider.activateDesc: Choose the direct/fallback model and review what changes before activating this provider.
provider.activateDirectModelDesc: Used for normal direct requests and whenever execution falls back to the active provider.
provider.activateRouterConflictDesc: Custom tiers reference another provider while cross-provider routing is off. Choose what should happen.
provider.activateUseRecommendedRoute: Use the new provider's recommended route
provider.activateUseRecommendedRouteDesc: Replace the current custom tiers with {provider}'s managed preset.
provider.activateKeepCustomRouteDesc: Preserve the tiers and explicitly enable cross-provider routing.
provider.activateDisableRouterDesc: Keep the saved tiers, but stop using them until routing is enabled again.
provider.activateRouterNeedsChoice: Choose how to resolve the provider conflict above.
provider.activateRouterLegacyPreserved: The existing route is preserved for compatibility.
provider.activateEnsembleOnPreserved: Stays on; members and aggregator are unchanged.
provider.modelDiscoveryFailed: Couldn't discover models. Enter a model id manually.
provider.upgradeGatewayHint: Update and restart the Gateway to use provider activation.
provider.discardDraftBody: Switching providers will discard the unsaved edits in this provider form.
provider.editingPrimary: Used for direct requests and as the primary fallback.
provider.editingNewPrimary: Saving will make this provider active for direct requests and primary fallback.
provider.editingProfile: Available to Routing and Ensemble; selecting it here does not make it active.
provider.editingNew: Saving makes this provider available to Routing and Ensemble; it will not become active.
provider.profileModelHint: This saved model becomes the direct/fallback model if you make this provider active. Routed models remain separate in Model Routing.
provider.removeConfirmBody: Remove {provider} from configured providers? Referenced providers cannot be removed.
provider.routingDesc: Configure tiers, ensemble, and single-model behavior in Model Routing.
provider.chooseToSeeFields: Choose a provider to see required fields.

recommendation.title: Recommended: TokenRhythm
recommendation.value: TokenRhythm API calls are free for a limited time.
recommendation.registration: During the promotion, register and get an API key to call DeepSeek, GLM, MiniMax, Kimi, and other leading models for free.
recommendation.cta: Register and get an API key
recommendation.externalLabel: Register and get an API key — TokenRhythm (opens in a new tab)
recommendation.stepPaste: Paste it into the API key field below
recommendation.stepSelectAndPaste: Choose Add provider, select TokenRhythm, then paste your API key
recommendation.stepReplaceAndPaste: Choose Replace key below, then paste your API key

provider.endpointDesc: These settings apply only when OpenSquilla calls {provider}.
provider.defaultModelGroupDesc: Used when Routing is off or when execution falls back to the active provider.
provider.configuredModelDescFixed: Fixed model mode: every request uses the model below.
provider.configuredModelDescRouter: Intelligent model routing mode: each request is routed to a suitable model and falls back to the model below when routing is unavailable.
provider.configuredModelDescEnsemble: Multi-model collaboration mode: several models generate together, falling back to the model below when the ensemble cannot complete.
provider.defaultModelDesc: Used for direct requests and primary fallback. Routed models are configured in Model Routing.
provider.defaultModelSemantics: A recommended model is prefilled when available. Choose or enter another model, then verify it before relying on it.
provider.profileModelGroupDesc: Saved with this provider and also used for configuration verification.
provider.profileModelDesc: Used if this provider becomes active. Routed models remain configured in Model Routing.
provider.runtimeDefaultsDesc: Gateway-wide behavior shared by every normal model request.
provider.timeoutDesc: How long to wait for a single model response before timing out — raise this for slow local models (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio).
provider.timeoutGlobalDesc: Gateway-wide timeout for normal model requests across all providers. Configuration verification uses a separate 30-second timeout.
provider.contextWindowDesc: Cap the prompt-assembly budget for this model. Leave blank to use the auto-detected window.
provider.contextWindowIdentityDesc: Applies wherever this exact provider/model pair runs, including Routing and Ensemble.
provider.contextWindowLocalWarning: Effective context window is {tokens} tokens. Local runtimes often default to a small window — raise the override if your model supports more.
provider.envMissing: {envKey} is not visible to this gateway process. Set it before starting or restarting the gateway, or paste an API key instead.
provider.envKeyFallback: the selected API key environment variable
provider.credentialSourceMissingEnv: Current source: environment variable {envKey} is not visible
provider.credentialSourceOptional: Current source: no API key configured (optional)
provider.credentialSourceNone: Current source: no saved API key
provider.credentialPublicHint: Current session can replace this credential but cannot view its secret.
provider.removeCredentialConfirmBody: Remove the credential saved for {provider} from this device and configuration? The provider, models, and routing settings will be kept. If it comes from an environment variable, the system environment variable itself will not be deleted.
provider.credentialWriteOnlyPlaceholder: Saved key kept — paste to replace
provider.optionalApiKeyHelper: Enter a key only if this endpoint requires authentication; otherwise leave it blank.
provider.credentialRevealUnavailable: Couldn't reveal the saved API key.
provider.connectionDesc: Sends a small model request using the current key, endpoint, and verification model, then validates that the stream completes. Provider charges may apply.
provider.verifiedConfigChanged: Configuration changed since verification — verify again
provider.streamIncompatible: ✗ Streaming response incompatible — The provider accepted the credential and model request, but returned an unsupported stream format.
provider.discoverFailed: Couldn't list models — type a model id.
provider.failureAuth: The provider rejected this API key.
provider.failureCredits: This account has no credits left.
provider.failureRateLimited: The provider is rate-limiting requests — try again in a moment.
provider.failureOverloaded: The provider is overloaded — try again in a moment.
provider.failureModelNotFound: The provider doesn't recognize this model id.
provider.modelSearchOrCustom: Search models or enter a custom ID
provider.modelNoMatches: No matching models — you can keep what you typed.
provider.modelListTruncated: Showing {shown} of {total} models — keep typing to narrow the list.
provider.modelProvenance: Live list from the provider · details from {sources}

preset.desc: Apply once to fill Model Routing; edit the active tiers there.
preset.customizedDesc: Active tiers are edited in Model Routing.

modelStrategy.title: Model routing
modelStrategy.desc: Choose how Squilla selects and uses configured models for new requests.
modelStrategy.modeDesc: Choose by outcome: intelligent routing balances token use and model capability; a fixed model keeps selection predictable; multi-model collaboration prioritizes capability for complex tasks and usually uses more tokens.
modelStrategy.providerFirstTitle: Add a model provider to start
modelStrategy.providerFirst: Model routing needs at least one saved provider. One provider is enough to route among its configured models.
modelStrategy.configuredProvidersOnly: Available providers and models come from Model Service.
modelStrategy.fallbackTierDesc: Use this default tier when routing cannot confidently determine the request level.
modelStrategy.modelRolesDesc: Define which model handles each request complexity level.
modelStrategy.advancedDesc: Change how routing information appears in chat without affecting model selection.
modelStrategy.visualModeDesc: Choose how the routing decision panel appears in chat replies. This does not change routing behavior.
modelStrategy.routerDependency: Choose models for each request level. One provider can supply every level.
modelStrategy.ensembleDependency: Uses {provider} credentials; provider default model is {model}.
modelStrategy.ensembleMinimum: Add at least one more candidate to benefit from ensemble routing.
modelStrategy.ensembleFailure: When fusion cannot proceed, fall back to the current model: {provider} · {model}.
modelStrategy.singleDependency: Choose the model used for every request.
modelStrategy.singleModelDesc: Used for every request in Fixed model mode, and as the fallback when routing or collaboration cannot complete.
modelStrategy.singleDesc: Use one predictable model without automatic routing or multi-model collaboration.
modelStrategy.crossProviderNotice: Cross-provider routing is active. Review each tier before saving.

router.title: Intelligent model routing
router.desc: Match each request to an appropriate model so simple tasks avoid unnecessary high-capability usage.

ensemble.title: Multi-model collaboration
ensemble.desc: Run multiple models in parallel and combine their work for complex tasks; this usually uses more tokens.

single.title: Fixed model
single.desc: Always use the model you choose, making model selection and usage easier to anticipate.

modelStrategy.openrouterFixedDesc: When your Model Service uses {provider}, ensemble routing uses four proposer models and one aggregator.
modelStrategy.ensembleEmpty: No candidate models yet. Save your Model Service settings to seed model tiers, or add candidates manually.
modelStrategy.ensemblePipeline: How it works: proposer models draft in parallel, then the aggregator fuses the drafts into the final reply. Only the aggregator can call tools.
modelStrategy.ensembleFlowDesc: Several models draft in parallel, then an aggregator combines their work into one final answer. This uses more model calls and may cost more.
modelStrategy.legacyDynamicNotice: This configuration uses the retired automatic ensemble mode. Convert it to a custom lineup to keep editing it here.
modelStrategy.schemePresetDesc: The provider's tuned lineup: four proposers plus one aggregator, ready to use.
modelStrategy.schemeCustomDesc: Build a role-based lineup from models available through the current provider.
modelStrategy.presetNoteDeepseek: Reasoning anchor — baseline drafts for complex tasks
modelStrategy.presetNoteGlm: Long-form and structured output — contrasts the DeepSeek family
modelStrategy.presetNoteKimi: Code and tool intent — covers programming requests
modelStrategy.aggregatorNote: Fuses every draft into the final answer; the only member that can call tools
modelStrategy.aggregatorSectionDesc: Exactly one aggregator produces the final answer. Pick the strongest model in your lineup.
modelStrategy.aggregatorInheritedNote: No aggregator assigned — the current chat model fuses the drafts
modelStrategy.proposerSectionDesc: Proposers draft in parallel; wall-clock time follows the slowest one.
modelStrategy.presetReadOnlyHint: This preset lineup is managed by the provider.
modelStrategy.presetProviderMismatchNotice: Turns still run the saved {presetProvider} preset, not {activeProvider}'s — reselect Preset to switch it to {activeProvider}.
modelStrategy.candidateDuplicate: This model is already in the proposer list.
modelStrategy.capacityWarn: {calls} model calls per turn — cost grows linearly and fusion gains taper past 4 proposers.
modelStrategy.capacityFull: Limit of {max} proposers reached — more drafts stop improving fusion; remove one before adding another.
modelStrategy.diversityHint: Several proposers share a model family — adding a different vendor usually improves fusion.
modelStrategy.effectiveFacts: Per turn: {calls} model calls · fuses once {quorum}/{proposers} proposers succeed · proposer timeout {proposerTimeout}s · aggregator timeout {aggregatorTimeout}s · {grace}s grace after quorum
modelStrategy.effectiveSummary: {proposers} proposers in parallel + 1 aggregator · about {calls} model calls per turn
modelStrategy.runtimeStrategyDesc: Success threshold, fallback, and time limits
modelStrategy.successThresholdAuto: Automatic · at least {quorum} of {proposers} return normally (allows 1 failure)
modelStrategy.runtimeLimits: Proposers {proposerTimeout}s · aggregator {aggregatorTimeout}s · {grace}s grace after the threshold

behavior.title: Session behavior
behavior.autoTitlesDesc: Generate short titles after the first message; off uses the first-message fallback.
behavior.statusOn: New sessions receive a short generated title after the first user message.
behavior.statusOff: New sessions keep the first-message fallback title without an extra naming call.

router.title: Model tiers
router.routingDisabledHint: Turn on AI single-model routing to edit model tiers.
router.routingEnsembleReadOnlyHint: AI ensemble routing is active. Standard model tier settings are temporarily inactive.
router.panelDesc: Tier models are sent through each row's provider, so every provider follows the same routing table shape.
router.providerFirst: Choose a provider first to preview and save model tiers.
router.ensembleProfileDesc: The model tier table supplies candidate models for AI ensemble routing.

ensemble.statusOn: AI ensemble routing is on — several models answer and one model synthesizes the final reply.
ensemble.allFailedFallback: Fall back to a single model

channels.title: Channels
channels.removeConfirmBody: {name} will be removed from the configuration. Restart required to take effect.
channels.nameLocked: Renaming isn't supported — use Duplicate as new
channels.fixRequired: Fill the highlighted required fields first

jump.description: Channels are set up on the channel workspace: pick a platform, fill the form, and test the connection right where the channel runs.
jump.summaryStatic: Add, edit, and test chat channels on the workspace.

aids.title: Feishu console shortcuts
aids.scopes_json: Permission manifest — paste into 权限管理 → 批量导入
aids.credentials_link: Open credentials page (App id / App secret)
aids.quick_apply_link: Pre-fill the permission request for all required scopes
aids.ws_order_note: You're connected — one step left in the Feishu console: open 事件与回调 and switch event delivery to 使用长连接接收事件. The console only saves that choice while a client is connected, which is why it comes after this save. This notice clears once the first event arrives.

search.title: Web search
search.multiProviderHint: Search automatically ranks across every provider whose API key is set. The provider chosen here supplies its inline key and default settings; set other providers' key environment variables to include them too.
search.statusOff: Web search is off until a provider is selected.
search.statusReady: Web search is ready for new turns.
search.statusNeedsKey: Web search is selected but still needs a visible provider key.

memory.title: Memory embedding
memory.autoCaptureDesc: Save highlights of your conversations into long-term memory.
memory.statusNone: Keyword search stays available; embeddings are disabled.
memory.statusLocal: Uses local BGE embeddings; no remote key is needed.
memory.statusOllama: Uses your Ollama server; no API key is needed.
memory.statusAuto: Local-first memory search; optional remote fallback can be configured.
memory.statusConfigured: Remote memory embeddings are configured. Restart required for changes to take effect.
memory.statusNeedsKey: Remote memory embeddings need a visible provider key before they can run.

image.title: Image generation
image.noKeyWhileDisabled: No key required while image generation is disabled.
image.statusDisabled: Image generation is hidden from agents until this capability is enabled.
image.statusReadyFallback: Image generation will be available in new turns using the same provider key.
image.statusReady: Image generation will be available in new turns once the gateway has the visible key.
image.statusNeedsKey: Image generation is enabled but still needs a visible provider key before agents can use it.
image.fallbacksHint: Comma-separated provider/model ids tried in order if the primary fails.

audio.title: Audio
audio.statusDisabled: Voice and audio tools are hidden from agents until this capability is enabled.
audio.statusReady: Voice and audio tools will be available in new turns once the gateway sees the key.
audio.statusNeedsKey: Audio is enabled but still needs an audio provider key before agents can use it.

connection.title: Connection
connection.desc: Point this browser at a gateway. Connecting applies immediately — no save needed.
connection.reasonConnected: Live link to the gateway socket.
connection.reasonDisconnected: Not connected. Check the WebSocket URL and try Connect.
connection.tokenDesc: Bearer token for gateways that require auth. Stored in this tab only.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.desc: The desktop app owns the local gateway and serves this Control UI from it.
runtime.resetDesc: Clear the saved credential and generated config, then re-run first-time setup on next launch.
runtime.noLogToReveal: No gateway log to reveal yet.
runtime.resetConfirmBody: This clears the saved desktop credential and generated config. The next launch re-runs first-time setup.
runtime.resetDone: Saved setup cleared. Restart the desktop app to re-run setup.

cleanup.label: Data cleanup
cleanup.desc: Review exact locations before changing this profile or deleting data. The installed app is never removed here.

reset-current-settings.title: Reset current profile settings
reset-current-settings.warning: Provider credentials and pending desktop setup records are cleared. Workspace identity, memory, and chat databases are kept.

delete-current-profile.title: Delete current profile
delete-current-profile.warning: The listed current profile, its credential, and its logs are deleted. Other recovery profiles and backups are kept.

delete-all-user-data.title: Delete all OpenSquilla user data
delete-all-user-data.warning: The primary profile, every recovery profile, credentials, logs, and recorded backups are deleted after OpenSquilla fully exits.

cleanup.revealFailed: Could not show the data location: {detail}
cleanup.blockedHelp: No data was changed by this inspection. Keep using OpenSquilla, show the data location, or retry after resolving the stable code above.
cleanup.acknowledge: I reviewed every listed location and understand that present items will be permanently deleted.
cleanup.typePhrase: Type {phrase} to confirm deletion of all user data.
cleanup.working: Stopping the local runtime and applying the confirmed cleanup…
cleanup.resetConfirmBody: This returns the current profile to provider onboarding while preserving workspace identity, memory, and chats.
cleanup.partial: Cleanup stopped partway through ({detail}). OpenSquilla preserved the remaining data and opened recovery options.
cleanup.deleteAllScheduled: OpenSquilla will now quit; deletion begins only after the app has fully exited.
cleanup.resetDone: Current settings were reset. Provider onboarding will reopen.
cleanup.deleteDone: The current profile was deleted. OpenSquilla will now quit.

runtime.uninstallDesc: Stop the local runtime and close the app. To remove the installed app itself, use your OS app removal flow after it closes.
runtime.uninstallConfirmBody: This stops the local runtime and closes the desktop app. Your sessions, config, and secrets stay on disk.
runtime.uninstallPurgeTitle: Delete local desktop data and quit?
runtime.uninstallPurgeBody: This stops the local runtime and permanently deletes the desktop profile on this machine: sessions, configuration, and secrets. It does not remove the installed app itself.
runtime.uninstallDone: Desktop cleanup complete. The app will now close; remove OpenSquilla through your OS if you want to uninstall the app itself.
runtime.migrationDesc: Use data from another OpenSquilla installation. You will review it first; if current Desktop data exists, OpenSquilla backs it up before replacing it.
runtime.migrationRecoveryProfile: Data transfer is unavailable in a recovery profile. Return to the main profile to continue.
runtime.migrationSummaryFailed: Could not review the selected data: {detail}
runtime.migrationChooseSourceDesc: Choose the data you want to use. Nothing is selected automatically. The two installations are never merged or synced, and the source stays unchanged. Choosing Not now does not affect your current data.
runtime.migrationOverwrite: Back up the current data, then replace it as a whole. Files and chats are never merged.
runtime.migrationConfirmBody: OpenSquilla stops the local runtime, copies the selected data, and restarts. The source stays unchanged. The two installations will not merge or sync.
runtime.migrationStarted: Validating and preparing the data transfer. Your current data has not changed.
runtime.migrationNotAppliedUnchanged: Your current sessions and data have not changed.
runtime.migrationAppliedRestartTitle: Data was replaced, but the local runtime did not recover
runtime.migrationAppliedRestartCommitted: The transfer was already committed. Do not run the transfer again.
runtime.migrationFailureLocked: A source data file is in use. Close the program using the source, then check it again.
runtime.migrationFailureChanged: The source data changed during transfer. Check the source again before retrying.
runtime.migrationFailureUnreadable: OpenSquilla could not safely read the selected source.
runtime.migrationFailureApply: OpenSquilla could not apply the transfer safely.
runtime.migrationFailureRestart: The transferred data is committed, but the local runtime is not healthy.
runtime.migrationCompleteCopied: Identity and memory, chats, settings, skills, media, and other data were copied.
runtime.migrationCompleteSource: The source data stays unchanged at {path}.
runtime.migrationCompleteIndependent: This Desktop installation is now independent and will not sync with the source.
runtime.migrationCompleteReplacement: The previous Desktop data is available in a complete backup. It was replaced as a whole; files and chats were never merged.

keyboard.title: Keyboard
keyboard.desc: Global shortcuts for this browser. Changes apply instantly — no save needed.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.desc: Experimental client preferences for this browser. They apply instantly — no save needed. Items marked
advanced.descReloadSuffix: take effect after a page refresh.
advanced.foldDesc: Build the streaming work card from an ordered event log. Turn off only to temporarily restore the compatibility renderer.
advanced.foldAria: New live turn renderer (takes effect after reload)
advanced.revealDesc: Hold the live answer's reveal in a [min, max] window so the router can decide first. min ≤ max.
advanced.approvalPollDesc: Restore the ~2s approvals poll as a self-healing fallback (default off; approvals already stream).
advanced.historyMergeDesc: On history sync, merge by message id and keep live-only fields (default off, transitioning on).
advanced.historyMergeAria: History reconcile-by-id merge (takes effect after reload)
advanced.agentConfigDesc: Manage existing agent configurations. This area will change in a future multi-agent redesign.
advanced.dataMaintenanceDesc: Review imports and backups. Desktop can also reset or delete local profile data.
advanced.runTraceDesc: Make log lines interactive and open a per-line node-step detail drawer in the Logs view.
advanced.runTraceAria: Run-trace drawer in Logs (takes effect after reload)

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.statusDisabledByEnv: Network observability is disabled by environment.
privacy.disableNetworkObservabilityDesc: Stop gateway network observability calls such as install telemetry and update checks.

toast.envNotVisibleGateway: {envKey} is not visible to this gateway process.
toast.providerCredentialExternalStillActive: Cleared {provider}'s saved credential reference, but system environment variable {envKey} is still providing a credential.
toast.providerActivatedRouterDisabled: {provider} is now active. Model Routing was turned off because its saved tiers use another provider.
toast.chooseProviderRouter: Choose a provider before saving model tiers.
toast.chooseFixedModel: Choose a fixed model before saving.
toast.channelSaved: Channel saved — configuration validated locally; use Test connection to verify credentials
toast.channelSavedLive: Channel saved and applied — running with the new configuration
toast.channelStartFailed: Channel saved, but it failed to start — open its status for the error, then retry with Restart
toast.memorySavedRestart: Memory saved. Restart required for embedding changes.
toast.envSavedRestart: {surface} saved ${envKey}. Start or restart the gateway with that variable set.
toast.envSavedReference: {surface} saved ${envKey} reference. Keep it set for gateway restarts.

needs.bundledLocal: Bundled local embeddings for the default path.
needs.noEmbedding: No embedding service; keyword search remains available.
needs.localOnnx: Local ONNX embedding assets from the suggested install.
needs.optionalRemoteFallback: Optional remote fallback credentials if configured.
needs.imageProviderModel: A provider/model id that supports image generation.
needs.optionalOnnxDir: Optional ONNX directory override for custom local assets.
needs.optionalLocaleHint: Optional locale hint such as zh-CN, en-US, or en-GB.
needs.offSurfacesError: off surfaces the original provider error.
needs.audioEnvVar: Environment variable to read for audio provider access.
needs.remoteEmbeddingKey: Remote embedding API key or env reference.

memoryLearning.dreamDesc: Periodically runs a model pass to consolidate session memory (every 24h). Model-routing self-learning relies on it for idle training windows.
memoryLearning.selfLearningDesc: Learns routing preferences from your corrections. Fully on-device — stores feature vectors, never raw conversation text.
memoryLearning.depDefault: Depends on memory consolidation above: turning this on enables it too; turning consolidation off alone keeps sampling but pauses automatic training.
memoryLearning.depLinked: Memory consolidation was enabled together with this. You can switch it off independently — self-learning keeps sampling, only automatic training pauses.
memoryLearning.depPaused: Memory consolidation is off — samples are still collected, but no automatic training runs. Re-enable it to resume.
memoryLearning.restartRequired: A gateway restart is needed for the schedule change to take effect.
memoryLearning.gatePausedDream: Training paused — waiting for memory consolidation to be re-enabled

gateReason.ready: Ready — will train at the next window
gateReason.cooldown: Cooling down after a recent training run
gateReason.insufficient_data: Not trained yet — not enough corrections
gateReason.insufficient_class_diversity: Not trained yet — corrections cover too few routing tiers

memoryLearning.detachedNote: The base routing weights were upgraded. Learning built on the old weights was archived; your corrections are unaffected and will retrain automatically on the new weights. No action needed.

name.label: Channel name
name.description: Unique identifier for this channel entry.

dingtalk.label: DingTalk
dingtalk.description: DingTalk corp robot via stream connection.

feishu.label: Feishu / Lark

domain.label: Region
domain.description: Pick the console that issued this App ID.

wecom.label: WeCom

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.subtitle: Start a task, unblock a run, or pick up where you left off.

empty.title: No sessions yet.
empty.body: Sessions appear here as agents pick up work.

delete.title: Delete session
delete.body:
Delete session "{title}"? This cannot be undone.

The transcript will not be flushed to disk; use /reset first if you want a backup.

taskInput.hint: Enter to start · Shift+Enter for a new line

inspect.copyKeyFailed: Could not copy the session key.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: Live status, recent sessions, and the event stream.
overview.noSessions: No sessions yet — open chat to start your first one.
overview.connHint: The gateway WebSocket URL and token now live in Settings.

action.title: Needs action
action.note: Fix these first to make OpenSquilla ready.

degraded.title: Degraded capabilities
degraded.note: OpenSquilla can run, but these capabilities need attention.

optional.title: Optional setup
optional.note: These improve capability or posture but do not block readiness.

gw.repairRemoteDetail: Start or repair the remote OpenSquilla gateway deployment, then refresh health.
gw.runLocalDoctorDetail: Checks local config and onboarding before restarting the gateway.

overview.diagnosePrompt: Please troubleshoot this OpenSquilla configuration using the client context and doctor report below. Prefer applicable in-app Settings. When hasTerminalWorkflow is false, do not suggest bare opensquilla commands; an owned Desktop gateway is managed by the app. Data transfer means backup followed by whole-data replacement, never a merge. An optional migration item is not a fault and may remain visible after transfer. Explain and guide only: do not migrate data, restart the gateway, write settings, or claim changes were performed.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: Custom personalities and skill sets you can chat with.
agents.settingsHint: Provider and model defaults live in Settings
agents.createHint: Created agents inherit the global default model. Click a card to view or edit details.
agents.emptyTextBefore: Use the form above to add one. The default
agents.customizeHint: Use as starting point for a new agent
agents.workspacePlaceholder: Leave blank to use the default path
agents.systemPromptHint: Stored in config — runtime currently sources from agent SOUL.md instead.
agents.deleteBody: Delete agent {id}? Existing chats with this agent will keep working but become unmanaged.
agents.discardBody: You have unsaved edits. Closing now will lose them.
agents.toastTweakId: Tweak the ID, then click Add to create your copy
agents.toastBuiltinImmutable: "{id}" is a built-in agent and cannot be modified.

channels.title: Channels
channels.subtitle: Runtime status for configured channels. Add, edit, and pair channels right here, or use the CLI.
channels.emptyMsg: No channels yet. Connect Slack, Telegram, Discord and more so your agent can chat where your team does.

editor.unknownType: No editor form is available for this channel type ({type}); edit the entry in the configuration file.
editor.entryMissing: Channel {name} was not found in the configuration.
editor.editIntro: Changes apply on Save. Blank secret fields keep their stored values.

compose.catalogFailed: The platform catalog could not be loaded: {error}

channels.probeNoDetail: The provider returned no additional detail.
channels.declarationEvidence: Declared by adapter; live proof pending
channels.noCapabilityEvidence: This adapter has not published capability evidence.
channels.secretRedactionHint: Stored secrets are never shown. Press Edit and use Replace to enter a new value.
channels.toastProbeUnsupported: {name} does not support a safe live probe
channels.toastEnabled: Enabled {name}; restart the gateway to apply
channels.toastDisabled: Disabled {name}; restart the gateway to apply

pairings.title: Members
pairings.description: Review who can start conversations with this channel. Approvals apply only to this channel.
pairings.emptyDescription: New requests and approved senders will appear here.
pairings.approveConfirmBody: Grant {sender} access to {channel}? The bot will let them know they can start chatting.
pairings.revokeConfirmBody: Remove {sender}'s access to {channel}? They will need approval before starting another conversation.
pairings.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or pairing code
pairings.asAdminHint: Approve with the full tool surface instead of member access.
pairings.membersHint: Members can chat and use safe tools. Admins get the full tool surface, with per-command confirmation for privileged actions.
pairings.adminGrantFailedAfterApprove: Approved {sender}, but the admin grant failed — retry from the members list.
pairings.approveAdminConfirmBody: Grant {sender} admin access to {channel}? They get the full tool surface, and the bot will let them know they can start chatting.
pairings.setAsAdminConfirmBody: Give {sender} the full tool surface on {channel}? Privileged actions still ask for per-command confirmation.
pairings.removeAdminConfirmBody: Return {sender} to member access on {channel}? They can still chat and use safe tools.
pairings.adminUpdateFailed: Could not update admin access for {sender}: {error}
pairings.asAdminBootstrap: This is me — make channel admin

channels.restartNotLoaded: This channel isn't loaded yet — restart the gateway to start it
channels.queryNotFound: Channel {name} was not found — it may have been removed.
channels.worksHint: Platform features this channel type supports, needs configuration for, or cannot do.
channels.maturityHint: How complete this adapter is relative to the provider's full API
channels.ownerNone: No gateway currently holds the connection
channels.staleData: Status may be stale — last updated {time}.
channels.unconfiguredHint: These channels are running in this gateway process but are not in the saved configuration.
channels.configActionHint: Writes configuration — takes effect after a gateway restart

home.galleryLead: Choose a platform to get started

detail.finalStepTitle: Final step in the Feishu console

view.title: Cron Jobs
view.subtitle: Time-driven tasks — orchestrate reminders, agent turns, and recurring work.

list.emptyMsg: No schedules yet. Create your first cron job to wake an agent, fire a reminder, or kick off recurring work — all on time, all on your terms.
list.noMatchesText: No schedules match your search. Try a different query, or clear it to see everything.

panel.name: Name
panel.timezoneHint: Leave empty to evaluate the cron expression in UTC. Example: {example1}, {example2}.
panel.wakeModeHint: Use {code} for main-session jobs that should ride the existing turn queue.
panel.bestEffort: Best-effort delivery (do not fail the job when delivery fails)

form.cronPreviewPlaceholder: Enter a 5-field cron expression to preview
form.cronParseError: Could not parse expression — expected 5 fields (m h dom mon dow).

jobModeHint.systemEvent: System events append text to the agent main session and wake the heartbeat.
jobModeHint.reminder: Static reminders deliver this message directly; no model call or scheduled agent turn is created.
jobModeHint.agentTurn: Agent tasks run as scheduled turns and use the selected session target.

sessionTargetHint.systemEvent: Main is locked for system events. Use Static Reminder for direct reminders.
sessionTargetHint.reminder: Static reminders run isolated and deliver back to the originating chat when one is available.
sessionTargetHint.current: The scheduled agent task continues in the active chat session.
sessionTargetHint.isolated: The scheduled agent task runs in its own cron session, separate from Main.
sessionTargetHint.session: The scheduled agent task continues in the named session key.
sessionTargetHint.default: Choose where this background agent task keeps its conversation context.

targetSessionHint.current: Current is bound to the active WebChat session key when the job is saved.
targetSessionHint.named: Use a full session key from the chat header.

form.toastIntervalInvalid: Interval must be an integer number of seconds

delivery.errWebhookUrlRequired: Webhook URL is required for webhook delivery
delivery.errFdChannelNeedsTarget: Failure destination channel needs a channel or recipient

skillsView.title: Skills
skillsView.subtitle: Composable agent capabilities: bundled OpenSquilla skills plus local managed, personal, project, and workspace packs.
skillsView.reloadPartial: Skill catalog reloaded at generation {generation} with {errors} error(s). {summary}
skillsView.reloadListFailed: The catalog reloaded, but the refreshed Skill list could not be loaded.
skillsView.reloadUnknownError: The Gateway kept the previous catalog.
skillsView.metaSkillsDesc: Composed workflows that drive a DAG of sub-skills.

autoPropose.title: Auto-Propose Settings
autoPropose.meta: Unattended synthesis of new meta-skills from your usage patterns.
autoPropose.scheduledHint: Run on {cron}. Drives the meta-skill-creator DAG against your top co-occurrence patterns.
autoPropose.dreamHint: Piggyback on the memory-dream completion. Independent of the cron toggle.
autoPropose.autoEnableHint: Promote only proposals that pass all gates and stay within the configured {risk} risk ceiling.
autoPropose.riskCeilingHint: Low is the default. Higher ceilings still run the static safety preflight and keep audit metadata.

autoEnabled.title: Auto-Enabled Meta-Skills
autoEnabled.meta: Promoted by auto-enable. Disable moves the skill back to pending proposals.

proposals.title: Pending Proposals
proposals.meta: meta-skill-creator candidates awaiting your accept/reject decision.
proposals.rejectBody: Reject and delete proposal {id}? This cannot be undone.
proposals.disableBody: Disable auto-enabled skill {name} and move it back to pending proposals?

skillDetail.notReadiness: Inferred items are advisory and do not affect readiness.
skillDetail.installUnavailable: No installer matches the current missing dependencies.

registry.hintBrowse: Search ClawHub skills to browse and install.
registry.hintGithub: Paste a GitHub skill URL above for direct install.

layerHelp.workspace: Workspace skills are local to the active workspace.
layerHelp.managed: Managed skills are locally installed into OpenSquilla state.
layerHelp.personal: Personal skills are local user installs, not bundled.
layerHelp.project: Project skills are local to the current project.
layerHelp.extra: Extra skills come from configured local directories.

skills.emptyFilter: No skills match the current filter.

stats.proposalsTitle: Pending meta-skill proposals - synthesised by meta-skill-creator from your usage patterns

monitorSupport.title: Support & diagnostics
monitorSupport.copyReportDescription: Share the current connection, configuration, and runtime status
monitorSupport.downloadBundleDescription: Includes logs, a configuration summary, and diagnostic results
monitorSupport.privacySummary: Conversation content is excluded by default and known credentials are redacted
monitorSupport.bundleSubtitle: Create a ZIP to share with OpenSquilla support.
monitorSupport.bundleScopeBody: Error and trace records cover no more than one day; runtime logs follow local rotation, so actual coverage may differ.
monitorSupport.bundleIncludeContentBody: Enable only when support explicitly asks; this may contain sensitive business information.
monitorSupport.bundleCredentialsBody: API keys, tokens, and other recognized secret values are redacted before packaging.

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Tokens, cost, and per-model spend across every session.

usageLogs.costHintTitle: CNY values use baked-in rate {rate}. Verify against current FX for accounting use.
usageLogs.nativeCostHintTitle: The original provider bill is in CNY; USD uses the fixed normalization rate recorded on the receipt.
usageLogs.nativeMixedCostHintTitle: Mixed-currency or pending billing stays in canonical USD; original confirmed receipts are shown separately.

coverage.approximate: Older Gateway: date ranges use approximate session-lifetime totals.
coverage.partial: Usage-ledger migration is still completing; all known usage is shown.
coverage.timezoneFallback: Timezone {requested} is invalid; dates are grouped in {effective}.
coverage.legacyIncluded: Pre-ledger usage is included in the total but cannot be assigned to a date.
coverage.unpriced: Known cost shown; {count} calls are unpriced, so cost is incomplete.
coverage.pendingBilling: {count} provider billing receipts are pending settlement.
coverage.nativeBillingMissing: {count} billed calls are missing native receipts.

chart.emptyWindow: No data in the selected window.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.emptyText: Run a session and token spend will appear here automatically.

breakdown.title: Model breakdown
breakdown.proratedNotice: Per-model split is estimated; total is the actual billed amount.

actual.label: Actual
actual.tooltip: Actual — cost billed by the provider

estimated.label: Estimated
estimated.tooltip: Estimated — derived locally from token counts

mixed.label: Mixed
mixed.tooltip: Mixed — partial billing data, rest estimated

unpriced.label: Unpriced
unpriced.tooltip: Unpriced — no pricing table entry for this model

ephemeral.label: Ephemeral
ephemeral.tooltip: Ephemeral session — cost not yet persisted

costSource.cacheBlindHint: Includes estimates that price cached tokens at the full input rate — treat as an upper bound.

logs.title: Logs
logs.subtitle: Live gateway log stream — filter and follow.
logs.statusUnavailableAria: Log status unavailable; log tailing can still work.
logs.statusUnavailableTitle: logs.status is unavailable; log tailing can still work.
logs.fileLogTitle: Gateway file logging is configurable via log_file_enabled, log_level, rotation settings, and OPENSQUILLA_LOG_DIR. Path: {path}.
logs.fileLogOffTitle: Logs are not written to disk; diagnostic bundles will lack history. Enable log_file_enabled in config. Path: {path}.
logs.rawTitle: Raw capture records full model requests and responses, including conversation content. Enable with OPENSQUILLA_TURN_CALL_LOG=1 or opensquilla diagnostics on --raw; turn off with opensquilla diagnostics off. Source: {source}. Directory: {path}.
logs.bundleBody: Collects gateway logs, error records, and redacted configuration into one zip you can attach to a bug report. Secrets and API keys are removed; conversation content is excluded unless you opt in below.
logs.noMatch: No lines match the current filter.

chat.placeholderLanding: Assign a task or ask anything
chat.aiDisclaimer: Content generated by AI may be inaccurate. For reference only; verify important information.
chat.aiGeneratedLabel: Content generated by AI, for reference only.
chat.attachFilesTitle: Attach files: any file type (images, PDF, Office, and text are read directly; other files go to the agent workspace)
chat.dropOverlayHint: Files will be added to the composer
chat.voiceUnavailableHint: Voice input isn't set up — enable it in Settings
chat.queueModeHint: Queue: the message waits and auto-sends when the current response finishes
